calendar.matters:
REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (474 27th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan J. Meckfessel for petitioners and co-trustees Debra J. Dolch and Melissa Chung, who were also present.
Zachary McCoy, court-appointed attorney for settlor Ok Soon Key (via Zoom).
Kathleen Tostanoski and Kevin Birmingham, listing agents.
Bianca Baca, buyers' agent.
Raul Luna and Alan Luna, buyers (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property located at 474 27th Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94121.

Mr. McCoy requested that the appearance of the settlor be waived. The Court granted the request.

The Court announced the initial bid amount of $2,670,000 and invited overbids. No overbids were received.

The Court confirmed the sale of the real property for $2,670,000, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the Report of Sale and granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property. No additional bond was required. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustees; For Authority To Sell Conservatee And Trust Beneficiary'S Personal Residence; And For Authority To E
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Advanced From Oct-27-25 To Oct-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustees; For Authority To Sell Conservatee And Trust Beneficiary'S Personal Residence; And For Authority To Enter Into Exclusive Right To Sell Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan J. Meckfessel for petitioners Debra Dolch and Melissa Chung, who were also present.
Zachary McCoy, court-appointed counsel for conservatee Ok Soon Key (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Order Appointing Successor Trustees, for Authority to Sell Conservatee and Trust Beneficiary's Personal Residence, and for Authority to Enter into Exclusive Right to Sell Agreement.

Mr. Meckfessel explained that insurance for the trust property had been obtained and that the current petition sought authority to sell the decedent's apartment building, which had served as her primary residence, in order to help fund her care needs. The Court inquired whether such authority could be exercised under the existing temporary letters. Mr. Meckfessel explained that the temporary appointment did not include authority to sell or list the property.

The Court also asked about notice to Patricia Key. Counsel reported that Ms. Key was difficult to reach, as she did not respond to phone calls, voicemails, or written correspondence, but could be contacted in person at her Sutter Street address. Mr. Meckfessel stated that notice had not been served for the underlying petition because the order advancing the hearing struck the portion requiring notice, which he interpreted as a waiver. Mr. McCoy confirmed that Ms. Key had received notice of prior proceedings in related matters, had never appeared, and that he would waive notice on behalf of his client if the Court required it.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
